LAW AND MOTION 302, A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT GUADALUPE GAMINO'S MOTION TO DISMISS PER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 583.310 IS GRANTED. FIVE YEARS FROM THE DATE OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT WAS FILED ELAPSED ON FEBRUARY 22, 2012 ONCE THE NECESSARY TIME PERIODS ARE EXCLUDED FROM COMPUTATION UNDER SECTION 583.340(C). DUE TO IMPOSSIBILITY, IMPRACTICABILITY, OR FUTILITY, THIS ACTION IS TOLLED DURING THE TIME WHEN THE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT WERE IN EFFECT (OCTOBER 31, 2007 THROUGH JULY 31, 2008) AND THE TIME WHEN THE DISMISSAL WAS IN EFFECT (AUGUST 31, 2011 THORUGH NOVEMBER 14, 2011). PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO SHOW THAT ANY FURTHER TOLLING IS WARRANTED. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077
MASTER COURT CALENDAR SET FOR APR-09-2012 VACATED. JUDGMENT OF DISMISSAL FILED 3/29/12. (206)

Notice Of Motion And Otion Tpo Dismiss Per Code Of Civil Procedure Section 583.310
Off Calendar Mar-29-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ALBERT LOEB BOASBERG, TEL. NO. 989-6960 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
GUADALUPE GAMINO, TEL. NO. 706-2090, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT GUADALUPE GAMINO'S Motion To Dismiss Per Code Of Civil Procedure Section 583.310 is Granted. Five years from the date of Plaintiff's Complaint was filed elapsed on February 22, 2012 once the necessary time periods are excluded from computation under section 583.340(c). Due to impossibility, impracticability, or futility, this action is tolled during the time when the default and default judgment were in effect (October 31, 2007 through July 31, 2008) and the time when the dismissal was in effect (August 31, 2011 thorugh November 14, 2011). Plaintiff has failed to show that any further tolling is warranted. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)

MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff Larry Pambid's Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Defendant is ordered to respond within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Plaintiff in the amount of $215, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Prevailing party to prepare the order. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-22-2008 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up.
Off Calendar Dec-27-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Judge Ollie Marie-Victoire, Clerk: Carla Amador & Sajja Ravinantapricha , CSR: Helen Soprep-Matley 225 (Department 218)

Appearances
Plaintiff, Larry Pambid, is present.
Attorney, Albert Loeb Boasberg, is present representing the plaintiff.

Defendant, Guadalupe Gamino, is not present.
(Default was entered on 10/31/07 as to the defendant)

This cause came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing. Larry Pambid is sworn and examined by Albert Loeb Boasberg. Plaintiff introduces a group of exhibits and are identified as plaintiffs exhibit 1.

After hearing the testimony and considering of the evidence presented, the Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff and against the Defaulted Defendant, Guadalupe Gamino, as follows:

Defendant shall pay plaintiff, as and for damages, the sum of $247,052.03 plus interest in the sum of $32,116.76 for a total Judgment of $279,168.79. Judgment by Court signed in open court.
